hook.text: Oil is finite. At some point production must peak and decline. The debate is when, not whether.

buildup.text: Peak oil is the moment when global oil production reaches its maximum rate before declining permanently.

discovery.text: M. King Hubbert predicted US oil would peak around 1970. It did. His method now applies to global forecasts.

twist.text: New technology keeps pushing the peak forward. Fracking and deep-sea drilling unlocked reserves once out of reach.

climax.text: Peak oil reminds us that fossil fuels are finite. Geology sets the ultimate limit on what we can extract.

punchline.text: Geology sets the limit; time enforces it.

quiz.question: What did M. King Hubbert correctly predict?
options: The discovery of oil in the Middle East
That US oil production would peak around 1970
